customSkills.name: minimax-text-enhancer
customSkills.description: Enhances text using prompt engineering for MiniMax models
customSkills.instructions:
# MiniMax Text Enhancer

## Trigger
This skill activates when you want to improve, refine, or optimize any text for use with MiniMax models. It applies established prompt engineering principles tailored to MiniMax's architecture and strengths.

## How It Works

When you invoke this skill, provide the text you want to enhance. The skill will:

1. **Analyze the input text** — Identify the core intent, task type, and any implicit requirements
2. **Apply MiniMax-optimized enhancements**:
   - Add clear, action-oriented instruction framing
   - Structure the prompt with explicit context and constraints
   - Include output format specifications when relevant
   - Remove ambiguity while preserving the original intent
   - Add necessary role or perspective assignments where helpful
   - Optimize token usage for efficiency without sacrificing clarity
3. **Output an enhanced version** ready for direct use

## Enhancement Principles Applied

- **Clarity**: Explicitly state what's needed rather than implying
- **Structure**: Use headers, bullet points, or numbered lists for complex tasks
- **Context**: Provide relevant background without overwhelming
- **Constraints**: Define boundaries, limits, or specific requirements
- **Format**: Specify exactly how output should be structured
- **Role assignment**: Assign clear persona or expertise when applicable
- **Examples**: Include few-shot examples for complex or ambiguous tasks

## Task Categories Handled

- General text/prompt enhancement
- Question reformulation for better answers
- Instruction refinement for specific tasks
- Conversation improvement
- Output format specification
- Context enrichment

## Usage

Simply paste your text and indicate you'd like it enhanced. The skill will return an optimized version with explanations of key improvements made.

use?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWe optimized \u003cstrong\u003eChatBrowserUse()\u003c/strong\u003e specifically for browser automation tasks. On avg it completes tasks 3-5x faster than other models with SOTA accuracy.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePricing (per 1M tokens):\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInput tokens: $0.20\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCached input tokens: $0.02\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOutput tokens: $2.00\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor other LLM providers, see our \u003ca href=\"https://docs.browser-use.com/supported-models\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003esupported models documentation\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eShould I use the Browser Use system prompt with the open-source preview model?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes. If you use \u003ccode\u003eChatBrowserUse(model='browser-use/bu-30b-a3b-preview')\u003c/code\u003e with a normal \u003ccode\u003eAgent(...)\u003c/code\u003e, Browser Use still sends its default agent system prompt for you.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou do \u003cstrong\u003enot\u003c/strong\u003e need to add a separate custom \"Browser Use system message\" just because you switched to the open-source preview model. Only use \u003ccode\u003eextend_system_message\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eoverride_system_message\u003c/code\u003e when you intentionally want to customize the default behavior for your task.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you want the best default speed/accuracy, we still recommend the newer hosted \u003ccode\u003ebu-*\u003c/code\u003e models. If you want the open-source preview model, the setup stays the same apart from the \u003ccode\u003emodel=\u003c/code\u003e value.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eCan I use custom tools with the agent?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes! You can add custom tools to extend the agent's capabilities:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" free?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes! Browser-Use is open source and free to use. You only need to choose an LLM provider (like OpenAI, Google, ChatBrowserUse, or run local models with Ollama).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eTerms of Service\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis open-source library is licensed under the MIT License. For Browser Use services \u0026amp; data policy, see our \u003ca href=\"https://browser-use.com/legal/terms-of-service\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eTerms of Service\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://browser-use.com/privacy/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePrivacy Policy\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow do I handle authentication?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCheck out our authentication examples:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/main/examples/browser/real_browser.py\"\u003eUsing real browser profiles\u003c/a\u003e - Reuse your existing Chrome profile with saved logins\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eIf you want to use temporary accounts with inbox, choose AgentMail\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTo sync your auth profile with the remote browser, run \u003ccode\u003ecurl -fsSL https://browser-use.com/profile.sh | BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=XXXX sh\u003c/code\u003e (replace XXXX with your API key)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThese examples show how to maintain sessions and handle authentication seamlessly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow do I solve CAPTCHAs?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor CAPTCHA handling, you need better browser fingerprinting and proxies. Use \u003ca href=\"https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github\u0026amp;utm_medium=readme-faq-captcha\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eBrowser Use Cloud\u003c/a\u003e which provides stealth browsers <p dir="auto"><strong>1. Create environment and install Browser-Use with <a href="https://docs.astral.sh/uv/" rel="nofollow">uv</a> (Python>=3.11):</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uv init && uv add browser-use && uv sync
# uvx browser-use install  # Run if you don't have Chromium installed"><pre>uv init <span class="pl-k">&&</span> uv add browser-use <span class="pl-k">&&</span> uv sync
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> uvx browser-use install  # Run if you don't have Chromium installed</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>2. [Optional] Get your API key from <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com/new-api-key?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-quickstart-api-key" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud</a>:</strong></p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="# .env
BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=your-key
# GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key"><pre class="notranslate"><code># .env
BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=your-key
# GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>3. Run your first agent:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="from browser_use import Agent, Browser, ChatBrowserUse
# from browser_use import ChatGoogle  # ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview')
# from browser_use import ChatAnthropic  # ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6')
import asyncio

async def main():
    browser = Browser(
        # use_cloud=True,  # Use a stealth browser on Browser Use Cloud
    )

    agent = Agent(
        task="Find the number of stars of the browser-use repo",
        llm=ChatBrowserUse(),
        # llm=ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview'),
        # llm=ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6'),
        browser=browser,
    )
    await agent.run()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">browser_use</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">Agent</span>, <span class="pl-v">Browser</span>, <span class="pl-v">ChatBrowserUse</span>
<span class="pl-c"># from browser_use import ChatGoogle  # ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview')</span>
<span class="pl-c"># from browser_use import ChatAnthropic  # ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6')</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">asyncio</span>

<span class="pl-k">async</span> <span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>():
    <span class="pl-s1">browser</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Browser</span>(
        <span class="pl-c"># use_cloud=True,  # Use a stealth browser on Browser Use Cloud</span>
    )

    <span class="pl-s1">agent</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Agent</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">task</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Find the number of stars of the browser-use repo"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">llm</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">ChatBrowserUse</span>(),
        <span class="pl-c"># llm=ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview'),</span>

    <span class="pl-k">await</span> <span class="pl-s1">agent</span>.<span class="pl-c1">run</span>()

<p dir="auto">We benchmark Browser Use across 100 real-world browser tasks. Full benchmark is open source: <strong><a href="https://github.com/browser-use/benchmark">browser-use/benchmark</a></strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Use the Open-Source Agent</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You need <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/customize/tools/basics" rel="nofollow">custom tools</a> or deep code-level integration</li>
<li>We recommend pairing with our <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/open-source/customize/browser/remote" rel="nofollow">cloud browsers</a> for leading stealth, proxy rotation, and scaling</li>
<li>Or self-host the open-source agent fully on your own machines</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Use the <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-hosted-agent" rel="nofollow">Fully-Hosted Cloud Agent</a> (recommended)</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Much more powerful agent for complex tasks (see plot above)</li>
<li>Easiest way to start and scale</li>
<li>Best stealth with proxy rotation and captcha solving</li>
<li>1000+ integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, and more)</li>
<li>Persistent filesystem and memory</li>
</ul>
<br>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🚀 Template Quickstart</h1><a id="user-content--template-quickstart" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🚀 Template Quickstart" href="#-template-quickstart"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Want to get started even faster?</strong> Generate a ready-to-run template:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uvx browser-use init --template default"><pre>uvx browser-use init --template default</pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This creates a <code>browser_use_default.py</code> file with a working example. Available templates:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>default</code> - Minimal setup to get started quickly</li>
<li><code>advanced</code> - All configuration options with detailed comments</li>
<li><code>tools</code> - Examples of custom tools and extending the agent</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">You can also specify a custom output path:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uvx browser-use init --template default --output my_agent.py"><pre>uvx browser-use init --template default --output my_agent.py</pre></div>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">💻 CLI</h1><a id="user-content--cli" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 💻 CLI" href="#-cli"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Fast, persistent browser automation from the command line:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="browser-use open https://example.com    # Navigate to URL
browser-use state                       # See clickable elements
browser-use click 5                     # Click element by index
browser-use type "Hello"                # Type text
browser-use screenshot page.png         # Take screenshot
browser-use close                       # Close browser"><pre>browser-use open https://example.com    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Navigate to URL</span>
browser-use state                       <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> See clickable elements</span>
browser-use click 5                     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Click element by index</span>
browser-use <span class="pl-c1">type</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Hello<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>                <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Type text</span>
browser-use screenshot page.png         <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Take screenshot</span>
browser-use close                       <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Close browser</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">The CLI keeps the browser running between commands for fast iteration. See <a href="/browser-use/browser-use/blob/main/browser_use/skill_cli/README.md">CLI docs</a> for all commands.</p>

<details>
<summary><b>What's the best model to use?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">We optimized <strong>ChatBrowserUse()</strong> specifically for browser automation tasks. On avg it completes tasks 3-5x faster than other models with SOTA accuracy.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Pricing (per 1M tokens):</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Input tokens: $0.20</li>
<li>Cached input tokens: $0.02</li>
<li>Output tokens: $2.00</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">For other LLM providers, see our <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/supported-models" rel="nofollow">supported models documentation</a>.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Should I use the Browser Use system prompt with the open-source preview model?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes. If you use <code>ChatBrowserUse(model='browser-use/bu-30b-a3b-preview')</code> with a normal <code>Agent(...)</code>, Browser Use still sends its default agent system prompt for you.</p>
<p dir="auto">You do <strong>not</strong> need to add a separate custom "Browser Use system message" just because you switched to the open-source preview model. Only use <code>extend_system_message</code> or <code>override_system_message</code> when you intentionally want to customize the default behavior for your task.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you want the best default speed/accuracy, we still recommend the newer hosted <code>bu-*</code> models. If you want the open-source preview model, the setup stays the same apart from the <code>model=</code> value.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use custom tools with the agent?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes! You can add custom tools to extend the agent's capabilities:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="from browser_use import Tools

tools = Tools()

@tools.action(description='Description of what this tool does.')
def custom_tool(param: str) -> str:
    return f"Result: {param}"

agent = Agent(
    task="Your task",
    llm=llm,
    browser=browser,
    tools=tools,
)"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">browser_use</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">Tools</span>

<span class="pl-s1">tools</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Tools</span>()

<span class="pl-en">@<span class="pl-s1">tools</span>.<span class="pl-c1">action</span>(<span class="pl-s1">description</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">'Description of what this tool does.'</span>)</span>
<span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">custom_tool</span>(<span class="pl-s1">param</span>: <span class="pl-smi">str</span>) <span class="pl-c1">-></span> <span class="pl-smi">str</span>:
    <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-s">f"Result: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">param</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>"</span>

<span class="pl-s1">agent</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Agent</span>(
    <span class="pl-s1">task</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Your task"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">llm</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">llm</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">browser</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">browser</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">tools</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">tools</span>,
)</pre></div>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use this for free?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes! Browser-Use is open source and free to use. You only need to choose an LLM provider (like OpenAI, Google, ChatBrowserUse, or run local models with Ollama).</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Terms of Service</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">This open-source library is licensed under the MIT License. For Browser Use services & data policy, see our <a href="https://browser-use.com/legal/terms-of-service" rel="nofollow">Terms of Service</a> and <a href="https://browser-use.com/privacy/" rel="nofollow">Privacy Policy</a>.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I handle authentication?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Check out our authentication examples:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/main/examples/browser/real_browser.py">Using real browser profiles</a> - Reuse your existing Chrome profile with saved logins</li>
<li>If you want to use temporary accounts with inbox, choose AgentMail</li>
<li>To sync your auth profile with the remote browser, run <code>curl -fsSL https://browser-use.com/profile.sh | BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=XXXX sh</code> (replace XXXX with your API key)</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">These examples show how to maintain sessions and handle authentication seamlessly.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I solve CAPTCHAs?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">For CAPTCHA handling, you need better browser fingerprinting and proxies. Use <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-faq-captcha" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud</a> which provides stealth browsers designed to avoid detection and CAPTCHA challenges.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I go into production?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Chrome can consume a lot of memory, and running many agents in parallel can be tricky to manage.</p>
<p dir="auto">For production use cases, use our <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-faq-production" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud API</a> which handles:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Scalable browser infrastructure</li>
<li>Memory management</li>
<li>Proxy rotation</li>
<li>Stealth browser fingerprinting</li>
<li>High-performance parallel execution</li>
</ul>
</details>
<br>

customSkills.name: browser-use-repair
customSkills.description: Diagnose and repair Browser Use MCP imports and Chromium setup.
customSkills.trigger: Use when a Browser Use import resolves with MCP servers: none, the browser-use MCP server is not running, Chromium is missing, or the CLI addon is missing.
customSkills.instructions:
When Browser Use was imported but no MCP server is visible, treat it as an import/installation problem, not as unavailable browser automation.
First import https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use through mcp__cowork__import_capability_link with enable_mcp=true, or ask the user to enable the saved browser-use MCP server in the MCP & Skills dashboard.
The expected MCP command is: uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use --mcp.
If startup reports that Chromium or Chrome is missing, install it with: uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use install.
If startup reports "CLI addon is not installed", keep the --from browser-use[cli] form; plain uvx browser-use --mcp can miss the CLI extra.
After changing the MCP config or installing Chromium, restart MCP servers from the dashboard and retry the Browser Use task.
If running inside Claude Code or Codex with shell access, diagnose with PowerShell commands such as Get-Command uvx and uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use --help before declaring failure.

skills.name: uvx-browser-use-install-Run-if-you-don-t-have-Chromium-installed
skills.trigger: When the user requests uvx-browser-use-install-Run-if-you-don-t-have-Chromium-installed behavior or this imported capability applies.

use?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWe optimized \u003cstrong\u003eChatBrowserUse()\u003c/strong\u003e specifically for browser automation tasks. On avg it completes tasks 3-5x faster than other models with SOTA accuracy.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePricing (per 1M tokens):\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInput tokens: $0.20\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCached input tokens: $0.02\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOutput tokens: $2.00\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor other LLM providers, see our \u003ca href=\"https://docs.browser-use.com/supported-models\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003esupported models documentation\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eShould I use the Browser Use system prompt with the open-source preview model?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes. If you use \u003ccode\u003eChatBrowserUse(model='browser-use/bu-30b-a3b-preview')\u003c/code\u003e with a normal \u003ccode\u003eAgent(...)\u003c/code\u003e, Browser Use still sends its default agent system prompt for you.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou do \u003cstrong\u003enot\u003c/strong\u003e need to add a separate custom \"Browser Use system message\" just because you switched to the open-source preview model. Only use \u003ccode\u003eextend_system_message\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eoverride_system_message\u003c/code\u003e when you intentionally want to customize the default behavior for your task.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you want the best default speed/accuracy, we still recommend the newer hosted \u003ccode\u003ebu-*\u003c/code\u003e models. If you want the open-source preview model, the setup stays the same apart from the \u003ccode\u003emodel=\u003c/code\u003e value.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eCan I use custom tools with the agent?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes! You can add custom tools to extend the agent's capabilities:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🤖 LLM Quickstart</h1><a id="user-content--llm-quickstart" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🤖 LLM Quickstart" href="#-llm-quickstart"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">👋 Human Quickstart</h1><a id="user-content--human-quickstart" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 👋 Human Quickstart" href="#-human-quickstart"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>1. Create environment and install Browser-Use with <a href="https://docs.astral.sh/uv/" rel="nofollow">uv</a> (Python>=3.11):</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uv init && uv add browser-use && uv sync
# uvx browser-use install  # Run if you don't have Chromium installed"><pre>uv init <span class="pl-k">&&</span> uv add browser-use <span class="pl-k">&&</span> uv sync
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> uvx browser-use install  # Run if you don't have Chromium installed</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>2. [Optional] Get your API key from <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com/new-api-key?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-quickstart-api-key" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud</a>:</strong></p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="# .env
BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=your-key
# GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key"><pre class="notranslate"><code># .env
BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=your-key
# GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>3. Run your first agent:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="from browser_use import Agent, Browser, ChatBrowserUse
# from browser_use import ChatGoogle  # ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview')
# from browser_use import ChatAnthropic  # ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6')
import asyncio

async def main():
    browser = Browser(
        # use_cloud=True,  # Use a stealth browser on Browser Use Cloud
    )

    agent = Agent(
        task="Find the number of stars of the browser-use repo",
        llm=ChatBrowserUse(),
        # llm=ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview'),
        # llm=ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6'),
        browser=browser,
    )
    await agent.run()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">browser_use</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">Agent</span>, <span class="pl-v">Browser</span>, <span class="pl-v">ChatBrowserUse</span>
<span class="pl-c"># from browser_use import ChatGoogle  # ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview')</span>
<span class="pl-c"># from browser_use import ChatAnthropic  # ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6')</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">asyncio</span>

<span class="pl-k">async</span> <span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>():
    <span class="pl-s1">browser</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Browser</span>(
        <span class="pl-c"># use_cloud=True,  # Use a stealth browser on Browser Use Cloud</span>
    )

    <span class="pl-s1">agent</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Agent</span>(
        <span class="pl-s1">task</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Find the number of stars of the browser-use repo"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s1">llm</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">ChatBrowserUse</span>(),
        <span class="pl-c"># llm=ChatGoogle(model='gemini-3-flash-preview'),</span>
        <span class="pl-c"># llm=ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6'),</span>
        <span class="pl-s1">browser</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">browser</span>,
    )
    <span class="pl-k">await</span> <span class="pl-s1">agent</span>.<span class="pl-c1">run</span>()

<p dir="auto">We benchmark Browser Use across 100 real-world browser tasks. Full benchmark is open source: <strong><a href="https://github.com/browser-use/benchmark">browser-use/benchmark</a></strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Use the Open-Source Agent</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You need <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/customize/tools/basics" rel="nofollow">custom tools</a> or deep code-level integration</li>
<li>We recommend pairing with our <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/open-source/customize/browser/remote" rel="nofollow">cloud browsers</a> for leading stealth, proxy rotation, and scaling</li>
<li>Or self-host the open-source agent fully on your own machines</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Use the <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-hosted-agent" rel="nofollow">Fully-Hosted Cloud Agent</a> (recommended)</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Much more powerful agent for complex tasks (see plot above)</li>
<li>Easiest way to start and scale</li>
<li>Best stealth with proxy rotation and captcha solving</li>
<li>1000+ integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, and more)</li>
<li>Persistent filesystem and memory</li>
</ul>
<br>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🚀 Template Quickstart</h1><a id="user-content--template-quickstart" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🚀 Template Quickstart" href="#-template-quickstart"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Want to get started even faster?</strong> Generate a ready-to-run template:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uvx browser-use init --template default"><pre>uvx browser-use init --template default</pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This creates a <code>browser_use_default.py</code> file with a working example. Available templates:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>default</code> - Minimal setup to get started quickly</li>
<li><code>advanced</code> - All configuration options with detailed comments</li>
<li><code>tools</code> - Examples of custom tools and extending the agent</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">You can also specify a custom output path:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="uvx browser-use init --template default --output my_agent.py"><pre>uvx browser-use init --template default --output my_agent.py</pre></div>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">💻 CLI</h1><a id="user-content--cli" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 💻 CLI" href="#-cli"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Fast, persistent browser automation from the command line:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="browser-use open https://example.com    # Navigate to URL
browser-use state                       # See clickable elements
browser-use click 5                     # Click element by index
browser-use type "Hello"                # Type text
browser-use screenshot page.png         # Take screenshot
browser-use close                       # Close browser"><pre>browser-use open https://example.com    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Navigate to URL</span>
browser-use state                       <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> See clickable elements</span>
browser-use click 5                     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Click element by index</span>
browser-use <span class="pl-c1">type</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Hello<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>                <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Type text</span>
browser-use screenshot page.png         <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Take screenshot</span>
browser-use close                       <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Close browser</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">The CLI keeps the browser running between commands for fast iteration. See <a href="/browser-use/browser-use/blob/main/browser_use/skill_cli/README.md">CLI docs</a> for all commands.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Claude Code Skill</h3><a id="user-content-claude-code-skill" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Claude Code Skill" href="#claude-code-skill"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">For <a href="https://claude.ai/code" rel="nofollow">Claude Code</a>, install the skill to enable AI-assisted browser automation:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/browser-use
curl -o ~/.claude/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/browser-use/browser-use/main/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md"><pre>mkdir -p <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/skills/browser-use
curl -o <span class="pl-k">~</span>/.claude/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/browser-use/browser-use/main/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md</pre></div>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Integrations, hosting, custom tools, MCP, and more on our <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com" rel="nofollow">Docs ↗</a></h2><a id="user-content-integrations-hosting-custom-tools-mcp-and-more-on-our-docs-" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Integrations, hosting, custom tools, MCP, and more on our Docs ↗" href="#integrations-hosting-custom-tools-mcp-and-more-on-our-docs-"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">FAQ</h1><a id="user-content-faq" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: FAQ" href="#faq"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details>
<summary><b>What's the best model to use?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">We optimized <strong>ChatBrowserUse()</strong> specifically for browser automation tasks. On avg it completes tasks 3-5x faster than other models with SOTA accuracy.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Pricing (per 1M tokens):</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Input tokens: $0.20</li>
<li>Cached input tokens: $0.02</li>
<li>Output tokens: $2.00</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">For other LLM providers, see our <a href="https://docs.browser-use.com/supported-models" rel="nofollow">supported models documentation</a>.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Should I use the Browser Use system prompt with the open-source preview model?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes. If you use <code>ChatBrowserUse(model='browser-use/bu-30b-a3b-preview')</code> with a normal <code>Agent(...)</code>, Browser Use still sends its default agent system prompt for you.</p>
<p dir="auto">You do <strong>not</strong> need to add a separate custom "Browser Use system message" just because you switched to the open-source preview model. Only use <code>extend_system_message</code> or <code>override_system_message</code> when you intentionally want to customize the default behavior for your task.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you want the best default speed/accuracy, we still recommend the newer hosted <code>bu-*</code> models. If you want the open-source preview model, the setup stays the same apart from the <code>model=</code> value.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use custom tools with the agent?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes! You can add custom tools to extend the agent's capabilities:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="from browser_use import Tools

tools = Tools()

@tools.action(description='Description of what this tool does.')
def custom_tool(param: str) -> str:
    return f"Result: {param}"

agent = Agent(
    task="Your task",
    llm=llm,
    browser=browser,
    tools=tools,
)"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">browser_use</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">Tools</span>

<span class="pl-s1">tools</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Tools</span>()

<span class="pl-en">@<span class="pl-s1">tools</span>.<span class="pl-c1">action</span>(<span class="pl-s1">description</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">'Description of what this tool does.'</span>)</span>
<span class="pl-k">def</span> <span class="pl-en">custom_tool</span>(<span class="pl-s1">param</span>: <span class="pl-smi">str</span>) <span class="pl-c1">-></span> <span class="pl-smi">str</span>:
    <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-s">f"Result: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">param</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>"</span>

<span class="pl-s1">agent</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">Agent</span>(
    <span class="pl-s1">task</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Your task"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">llm</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">llm</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">browser</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">browser</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">tools</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">tools</span>,
)</pre></div>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Can I use this for free?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Yes! Browser-Use is open source and free to use. You only need to choose an LLM provider (like OpenAI, Google, ChatBrowserUse, or run local models with Ollama).</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>Terms of Service</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">This open-source library is licensed under the MIT License. For Browser Use services & data policy, see our <a href="https://browser-use.com/legal/terms-of-service" rel="nofollow">Terms of Service</a> and <a href="https://browser-use.com/privacy/" rel="nofollow">Privacy Policy</a>.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I handle authentication?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Check out our authentication examples:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/main/examples/browser/real_browser.py">Using real browser profiles</a> - Reuse your existing Chrome profile with saved logins</li>
<li>If you want to use temporary accounts with inbox, choose AgentMail</li>
<li>To sync your auth profile with the remote browser, run <code>curl -fsSL https://browser-use.com/profile.sh | BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=XXXX sh</code> (replace XXXX with your API key)</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">These examples show how to maintain sessions and handle authentication seamlessly.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I solve CAPTCHAs?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">For CAPTCHA handling, you need better browser fingerprinting and proxies. Use <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-faq-captcha" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud</a> which provides stealth browsers designed to avoid detection and CAPTCHA challenges.</p>
</details>
<details>
<summary><b>How do I go into production?</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">Chrome can consume a lot of memory, and running many agents in parallel can be tricky to manage.</p>
<p dir="auto">For production use cases, use our <a href="https://cloud.browser-use.com?utm_source=github&utm_medium=readme-faq-production" rel="nofollow">Browser Use Cloud API</a> which handles:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Scalable browser infrastructure</li>
<li>Memory management</li>
<li>Proxy rotation</li>
<li>Stealth browser fingerprinting</li>
<li>High-performance parallel execution</li>
</ul>
</details>
<br>
<div align="center" dir="auto">

customPlugins.name: browser-use
customPlugins.description: Browser Use MCP server and repair guidance for local browser automation.

skills.name: browser-use-repair
skills.description: Diagnose and repair Browser Use MCP imports and Chromium setup.
skills.trigger: Use when a Browser Use import resolves with MCP servers: none, the browser-use MCP server is not running, Chromium is missing, or the CLI addon is missing.
skills.instructions:
When Browser Use was imported but no MCP server is visible, treat it as an import/installation problem, not as unavailable browser automation.
First import https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use through mcp__cowork__import_capability_link with enable_mcp=true, or ask the user to enable the saved browser-use MCP server in the MCP & Skills dashboard.
The expected MCP command is: uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use --mcp.
If startup reports that Chromium or Chrome is missing, install it with: uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use install.
If startup reports "CLI addon is not installed", keep the --from browser-use[cli] form; plain uvx browser-use --mcp can miss the CLI extra.
After changing the MCP config or installing Chromium, restart MCP servers from the dashboard and retry the Browser Use task.
If running inside Claude Code or Codex with shell access, diagnose with PowerShell commands such as Get-Command uvx and uvx --from browser-use[cli] browser-use --help before declaring failure.
